skill.name: ralph-tui-create-beads
skill.description: Convert PRDs into Ralph TUI bead tasks. The skill creates an epic, child beads for user stories, quality gates, and dependency order.
skill.summary: Convert PRDs into executable Ralph TUI bead task plans.
skill.author: subsy

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many external command patterns and weak cryptography patterns. The cryptography matches are false positives from markdown prose and examples, while the command patterns are real shell examples for bd and ralph-tui usage. No malicious intent, network access, secret access, or prompt injection was found, but generated shell commands from PRD text need user review before execution.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Generation From PRD Content
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to create bd shell commands using PRD-derived titles and descriptions. The single-quoted heredoc pattern reduces shell expansion risk, but generated commands should still be reviewed before execution because untrusted PRD text could affect command structure if delimiters are mishandled.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are clearly present and they include PRD-derived descriptions. The skill also documents a safer heredoc pattern, so this is a controlled workflow concern rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Static Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but the reviewed SKILL.md content contains no cryptographic operations. The cited lines are markdown metadata, examples, and workflow guidance for creating Ralph TUI beads.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no hashing, encryption, or crypto API usage at the reported locations. The static pattern appears to have matched benign markdown text and command examples.

dangerous_patterns.title: Command Substitution Used For Heredoc Arguments
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented bd create examples use command substitution with cat and heredoc text to pass multiline descriptions. This is a legitimate shell pattern, but it is still a dangerous pattern if generated from untrusted text and executed without review.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly visible in the skill examples. The stated purpose is legitimate task creation, but the use of shell evaluation keeps the risk above low.

dangerous_patterns.title: External CLI Commands In Skill Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill tells users to run bd create, bd dep add, and ralph-tui run commands. These commands are expected for this tool, but marketplace users should understand that the workflow modifies local bead task files and invokes external CLIs.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly documented and match the advertised Ralph TUI workflow. They are not hidden or obfuscated, so the concern is disclosure and safe execution rather than malicious intent.

content.user_title: Convert PRDs Into Ralph TUI Beads
content.value_statement: PRDs often need careful task breakdown before autonomous execution can start. This skill turns PRD stories into ordered Ralph TUI beads with quality gates and dependencies.
actual_capabilities: Extracts quality gates from a PRD and applies them to bead acceptance criteria.
Creates an epic bead that links back to the source PRD.
Turns each user story into a focused child bead for Ralph TUI execution.
Orders stories by dependency, such as schema before backend before UI.
Adds bead dependencies with bd dep add after bead creation.
Identifies oversized stories and guides the user to split them.
limitations: Requires the bd CLI and Ralph TUI workflow to be available in the project.
Does not validate that generated commands succeeded after execution.
Depends on clear PRD structure and explicit user stories for best results.
Generated shell commands should be reviewed before running on untrusted PRDs.

use_cases.title: Prepare PRDs For Ralph TUI
use_cases.description: Convert a product requirements document into an epic and child beads ready for Ralph TUI execution.

use_cases.title: Break Down Large Features
use_cases.description: Split broad requirements into smaller beads that fit one autonomous agent iteration.

use_cases.title: Apply Quality Gates Consistently
use_cases.description: Attach required type checks, lint checks, and UI verification steps to every relevant story.

prompt_templates.title: Convert A PRD
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for a single PRD file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this PRD into Ralph TUI beads. Create one epic, child beads for each user story, and include quality gates from the document.

prompt_templates.title: Split Oversized Stories
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate workflow for broad or unclear feature requests.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this PRD and split any oversized stories before creating beads. Keep each bead small enough for one Ralph TUI iteration.

prompt_templates.title: Add Dependency Order
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for features with backend and UI sequencing.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create beads from this PRD and order them by dependency. Put schema work first, backend work next, UI work after that, and add dependency commands.

prompt_templates.title: Create Production-Ready Beads
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow with quality gates and verification rules.
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this PRD into production-ready Ralph TUI beads. Include acceptance criteria, quality gates, dependency ordering, priorities, and a final checklist.

output_examples.input: A PRD for adding an investor type filter with schema, UI, and dashboard changes.
output: An epic bead for the feature with a link to the source PRD.
Child beads for schema, list UI, filtering, and dashboard updates.
Dependencies that ensure UI work waits for backend and schema work.

output_examples.input: A PRD that includes typecheck, lint, and browser verification quality gates.
output: Acceptance criteria that include story-specific requirements.
Universal quality gates added to every bead.
Browser verification added only to UI-related beads.

best_practices: Review generated commands before running them, especially when the PRD came from an untrusted source.
Keep each bead small enough for one Ralph TUI iteration.
Place dependency-building stories before the stories that rely on them.
anti_patterns: Creating one large bead for an entire feature area.
Omitting quality gates from acceptance criteria.
Running generated shell commands without reviewing titles and descriptions first.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a Ralph TUI epic and child beads from a PRD or PRD text.

faq.question: Does it run Ralph TUI automatically?
faq.answer: No. It prepares bead creation commands and shows how to run Ralph TUI after beads exist.

faq.question: How does it handle quality gates?
faq.answer: It extracts quality gates from the PRD and appends them to each relevant bead.

faq.question: Can it split large user stories?
faq.answer: Yes. It tells the assistant to split stories that are too large for one Ralph TUI iteration.

faq.question: What should I review before running commands?
faq.answer: Review generated titles, descriptions, heredoc delimiters, priorities, and dependency commands.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
